title: Crocus’s Life Inside Laboon
description: On pages 1–3, crocus’s life inside laboon as crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain.
scene: Crocus’s Life Inside Laboon. Crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: This is some waterway.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm amazed you've managed to dig out such a big hole and live inside here.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Is this another one of your ways of having a good time?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's a hobby of mine as a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Don't get the wrong idea. I may not look like much but I'm a real doctor. I even used to run a clinic at the cape long ago.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Not only that, I've got a few years under my belt working as a ship doctor too.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: A Doctor!? Really!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Then join my crew as a doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Stop talking nonsense,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'm too old to be doing reckless things unlike you youths.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So you're a doctor, huh... and you're living inside a whale?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's right. With a whale this big, it's impossible to properly treat it from the outside.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'll open the gate now.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Alright!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: We're out! I can see the real sky at last!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: What should we do with these two?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Just throw them over somewhere around here.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: shut!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Ubagh!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Where are we!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Is this stomach acid!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: No, we appear to be in a real ocean, Ms. Wednesday.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: We must've been knocked out by those pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 9
dialogue.text: MR. 9!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: So... who were you guys again?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Shut up! It's none of your business!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: No wait, Miss Wednesday. Perhaps it is their business. As long as they're pirates, that is:

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 9
dialogue.text: True indeed, Mr. 9. Our organization may have some use for you.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Prepare your- selves!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Then we bid you good day until next time, you hillbilly pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: And heed this, CroCus! That whale shall be ours for the taking soon enough!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: So her name is Ms. Wednesday? What a beautiful enigma, you are.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Huh? Did someone drop this or something?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Bye-bye, base

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Are you sure about just letting them go? They'll be sure to come back for the whale again, you know.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Even if I didn't let them go, there'll be other Rogers who'll come to take their places.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on crocus’s life inside laboon. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
